references.notes: The phrase 'ريب المنون' (the vicissitudes of fate/death) refers to fate as a cosmological force, a common pre-Islamic concept.
The phrase 'لعمروك' (by your life) is a common pre-Islamic oath formula, anchoring the oath to the sacredness of life.
The phrase 'ريب المنون' (the vicissitudes of fate/death) again refers to fate as an inescapable cosmological force.
The term 'منايا' (fates/deaths) refers to destiny and the inevitability of death, aligning with pre-Islamic cosmological views on fate.
The verse explicitly mentions "دواهي الدهر" (calamities of Dahr), referring to Dahr as a powerful, often destructive, cosmological force in pre-Islamic belief.
